description:
Walk every spec body post-typer and rewrite `Term::CallClosure(v, args, cont)` → `Term::Call(F, args, cont)` (and `TailCallClosure` → `TailCall`) when `fn_constants[v] = F`.

Depends on .29.10.1 (fn_constants), .29.10.2 (narrow spec registration).

## Scope

- New helper in `src/ir_rewrite.rs` (or extend an existing module): `pub fn rewrite_known_target_closures(module: &mut Module, types: &ModuleTypes)`.
- Per-spec walk: clone-rebuild block terminators where the closure var has a known FnId.
- Driver wires the pass after `type_module` and before codegen. All three execution paths share the lowered IR, so a single rewrite covers interp/JIT/AOT (three-path parity).

## Tests (TDD)

- `closure_call_rewritten_to_direct_call`: assert apply2's specialized body has `Term::Call(double, …)`, no `Term::CallClosure`.
- `tailcall_closure_variant_rewritten`: same for `TailCallClosure`.
- `flip_higher_order_callee_test`: rename `higher_order_callee_keeps_any_key_for_fn_as_value` → `_drops_any_key`, assert the any-key is now ABSENT.
- Full suite + spec-parity fixtures stay green.

## Out of scope

- Multi-target rewrite (when fn_constants would be a Set<FnId>) — separate ticket.
title: IR rewrite known-target CallClosure to direct Call
